title: Do Not Stop Sea Turtle Fights

content.en: Some divers were at a beach in Xiaoliuqiu. They heard loud splashes! They saw two big things push each other. The things bit each other. They hit each other. Were monsters fighting?!
content.tw: 在小琉球的沙灘邊，幾名潛水客聽到很大的潑水聲。他們發現有兩個大東西正在互相推擠。牠們咬來咬去，攻擊對方，是怪物在打架嗎?

content.en: They were not monsters. They were sea turtles! Maybe they were fighting for space. Sea turtles like to have their own space.
content.tw: 其實牠們不是怪物，而是兩隻海龜。牠們也許在搶地盤，因為海龜喜歡有自己的空間。

content.en: The divers were worried. But they did not stop the fight. Why not? Well, in Taiwan, we cannot touch sea turtles.
content.tw: 潛水客擔心海龜受傷，並沒有出手阻止。為什麼呢？因為在台灣，人是不能碰海龜的！

content.en: Taiwan wants to keep sea turtles safe. There are not many now. Touching sea turtles can hurt them. Their skin has sticky stuff on it. When people touch this stuff, sea turtles can get sick. And they can get scared and swim into something dangerous.
content.tw: 台灣想保護為數不多的海龜。但是人類碰到海龜的皮膚，可能會導致海龜生病，或是驚嚇到牠，害牠亂了方向，撞上危險的東西。

content.en: So people who touch sea turtles may have to pay a big fine! For one turtle, the fine is three hundred thousand Taiwan dollars. For two, it is six hundred thousand Taiwan dollars! This means not touching sea turtles is very important!
content.tw: 如果有人碰了海龜，就要付高額的罰款。碰一隻海龜可能會罰到三十萬元，如果碰了兩隻，罰金還會加倍！所以，千萬不要碰海龜喔！

content.en: Sea turtles need help. This time, the divers helped by not stopping the fight!
content.tw: 海龜需要幫助。這次，潛水員們透過不阻止打鬥來提供幫助！

vocabulary.preface: 海龜非常可愛，在台灣被列為保育類動物，需要保護。

content.text: 1. swim 游泳. The sea looks perfect today! 今天的海看起來真完美! I can't wait to swim! 我等不及要游泳了! Are you sure? 你確定嗎? I heard there are turtles in this area.聽說這個區域有海龜。
2. hurt 傷害. Wait, so I can swim with turtles?! 等等，我可以跟海龜一起游!? That would be amazing! 太棒了! Don't touch them, though. You might hurt them. 可不要碰牠，可能會有傷害。
3. push 推擠. I'd never hurt a turtle! 我絕對不會傷害烏龜! I'm an animal lover! 我愛護動物! The last time I saw you, 我上次看到你， you were pushing two ants to make them fight. 你正在催兩隻螞蟻打架。
4. fight 打架. They were NOT fighting. 牠們不是在打架。They were just… talking.只是在講話。

vocabulary.postscript: 今天的單字都是動詞。swim游泳 hurt傷害 push推擠 fight打架

quiz.question: Why is it important not to touch sea turtles?
How much is the fine for touching one sea turtle?
options: c. Three hundred thousand Taiwan dollars

content.en: Today is the last day of the Lunar New Year holiday. That means it is the first day of the Taiwan Lantern Festival in Chiayi!
content.tw: 今天是農曆新年的最後一天，這也代表嘉義的台灣燈會正式開始了！

content.en: There is someone new at the festival. He is famous! He is short! He has blue eyes and brown hair! He has a big mustache under his big nose! He wears blue overalls and a red cap. The letter "M" is on his cap. Oh, and he is from a video game. Who is he?
content.tw: 今年的燈會出現了一位家喻戶曉的角色。他個子不高，有藍色眼睛、棕色頭髮，還有大鬍子！他穿著藍色吊帶褲，戴著紅色帽子，帽子上寫了字母「M」。你猜到是誰了嗎？

content.en: This famous guy is in one part of the festival. But the festival has sixteen parts! They all have amazing lanterns.
content.tw: 這個角色出現在燈會的其中一個展區。燈會一共有十六個展區，每區都有很精彩的花燈！

content.en: Some lanterns show cool stories of the Tsou people. Some show the delicious food Chiayi grows. Some show new things made by its young people.
content.tw: 有一些燈籠呈現鄒族的故事，有些介紹嘉義的在地美食，還有一些展示出年輕人的創意！

content.en: Other lanterns show how Chiayi is changing. And some show how Chiayi cares about the Earth.
content.tw: 從燈籠中，能看見嘉義的發展與轉變，一些環境保護相關的花燈也展現出嘉義人的生態關懷。

content.en: The festival has Taiwan's exciting lantern contest too!
content.tw: 燈會現場還能看到台灣花燈比賽！

content.en: You can see all this until March fifteenth. Someone wearing blue overalls and a red cap is waiting for you there! Happy Lantern Festival!
content.tw: 這些精彩內容你都能欣賞到三月十五日。穿著藍色吊帶褲、戴著紅色帽子的人正在那裡等你喔！元宵節快樂！

vocabulary.preface: 農曆春節假期之後，大家就要準備吃元宵賞花燈了。

content.text: 1. part 部分。I love Lantern Festivals! 我喜歡燈節!There's something magical about them. 它有一種魔力。Totally agree. 我完全同意。I think it's the best part of the holiday season. 我覺得它是春節最棒的部分。
2. make 製作。Last year I even saw several lanterns made by inmates.我去年還看到幾個囚犯做的花燈。Can you imagine that? 你能想像嗎?
3. change 改變。Wow, sitting in jail and making lanterns…哇，坐在監獄裡，動手做花燈...That really changed the way I think about inmates.真的讓我改變了對囚犯的想法。
4. story 故事。Yeah, it would make a great story, wouldn't it? 這會是很好的故事，不是嗎?Inmates, lanterns, light, hope…囚犯，花燈，光明，希望...

vocabulary.postscript: 你記得剛剛聽到的單字嗎?part部分 make製作 change改變 story故事

quiz.question: 1. Where is the Taiwan Lantern Festival?
2. What does the famous guy have?
3. How many parts does the festival have?

title: A Pole to Poke with Snack Sticks

content.en: The Dongnan Night Market is in Yunlin. Many food stalls there sell delicious snacks on bamboo sticks.
content.tw: 在雲林的東南夜市裡，很多攤販會賣插在竹籤上的美味小吃。

content.en: Some food stalls sell corn on sticks. Some sell meat on sticks. Others sell sweet potato balls on sticks.
content.tw: 賣烤玉米的，烤肉串的，或是地瓜球的攤販，都會用到竹籤！

content.en: When some people finish their snacks, they drop their sticks on the ground. Bamboo sticks are sharp. Other people step on them. The sticks can hurt their feet!
content.tw: 很多人吃完東西後，就把竹籤隨手丟在地上。在人來人往的夜市中，地上尖尖的竹籤可能會刺傷人！

content.en: One man saw people getting hurt. His food stall does not sell snacks on sticks. But he wanted to keep people safe. He had a smart idea. He built a tall pole with straw and wood.
content.tw: 一名攤販發現有人因為竹籤受傷，想到了一個聰明的解決辦法。他豎起一根用稻草跟木頭做的柱子！

content.en: When people finish eating, they poke their sticks into the pole. Now fewer sticks are on the ground. The night market is cleaner. Walking is safer.
content.tw: 大家吃完東西之後，可以把竹籤戳進這個柱子裡面。這樣一來，地上的竹籤減少了，夜市變得比較乾淨，走路也比較安全！

content.en: People like the pole. Many say it is simple and helpful. Some say it is fun. Others say it could be made into a straw man! The man says he will think about it.
content.tw: 大家很喜歡這個簡單、有趣的柱子，還有人提議要把柱子做成稻草人的形狀！

content.en: One man's simple idea is making the night market safer and cleaner for everyone! Now go and poke the pole!
content.tw: 一位男士的簡單想法正在讓夜市對每個人來說更安全、更乾淨！現在去戳戳那根柱子吧！

vocabulary.preface: 尖尖的竹籤可能造成傷害，用心的攤販主人想辦法改變大家的使用習慣。

content.text:
1. people 人群。
You’re late. 你遲到了。
Sorry, the night market was full of people.真抱歉，夜市擠滿了人。
I had to stand in line for everything. 每樣東西都得排隊等。

2. food 食物。
But look what I got. 不過你看我買的。
Wow, pig’s ears and chicken feet! 豬耳朵，還有雞腳!
Did you get them from that food stall I told you about?
你是在我跟你講的那一攤買的嗎?
Yep, that’s the one. 對，就是那家。

3. corn 玉米。
And I got some corn too. 我還買了幾支玉米。
Corn is the best snack! 玉米是最棒的零食!

4. sweet potato 地瓜。
I heard their sweet potatoes are good too. 聽說他們的地瓜也不錯。

vocabulary.postscript:
你喜歡逛夜市嗎?來讀單字。
people人群 food食物 corn玉米 sweet potato地瓜

quiz.question: 1. What do some people do with their sticks at the night market?
options: b. Drop them on the ground

quiz.question: 2. Why did the man make the pole?
3. What is the pole made of?

title: Man Cleans Clothes for People with No Home

content.en: Clean clothes make us feel good. They smell good. They make us look good. And they feel good on us!
content.tw: 穿著乾淨的衣服會讓人心情變好。乾淨的衣服既舒服又好看！

content.en: An American man was a police officer. He helped people. He is not a police officer now. But he wants to keep helping people. The man helps people who do not have a home. It is hard for them to wash their clothes.
content.tw: 有一名退休的美國警察喜歡幫助有需要的人，尤其是無家可歸的人，因為他們沒有辦法洗衣服！

content.en: The man has a bus. He put three washing machines and three clothes dryers in the bus. He drives to the same places every week. People know when and where to find him.
content.tw: 於是，他在一輛巴士上裝了三台洗衣機跟三台烘衣機。每個星期，他都會把公車開到固定的地點，方便大家找他洗衣服。

content.en: The man washes, dries, and folds these people's clothes for free! In about three months, the man washed more than nine hundred kilograms of clothes! And these clean clothes helped some people find a job.
content.tw: 他會免費為那些無家可歸的人洗衣服、烘乾，然後摺好！在短短的三個月內，他洗了超過九百公斤的衣服。穿上乾淨的衣服之後，有一些人甚至找到了工作！

content.en: Now the man wants another bus. He wants more washing machines and clothes dryers. He needs people to drive the bus. They will wash clothes for schoolkids who do not have a home.
content.tw: 現在，他希望有第二輛公車放更多洗衣機跟烘衣機，也想找司機來幫忙開公車。這樣一來，就能為無家可歸的學童洗衣服。

content.en: Everyone feels good wearing clean clothes. This man's idea helps many people feel good!
content.tw: 每個人穿著乾淨的衣服都會感覺很好。這名男子的想法幫助許多人感覺良好！

vocabulary.preface: 能夠穿乾淨的衣服是很大的福氣，一名好心人免費幫街友洗衣。

content.text:
1. wash 洗。
I have so many clothes to wash! 我有好多衣服要洗!
That’s exactly what a washing machine is for. 洗衣機正是做這件事用的。

2. dry 弄乾。
I know, but I still have to dry them and fold them.
我知道啊，可是我還得把它烘乾，摺起來。
Come on, let me give you a hand.來吧，我來幫你忙。

3. clean 乾淨的。
Aw, that’s so nice of you! 你真好!
I’m happy to help. 我很樂意幫忙。
Clean clothes always smell and feel good. 乾淨衣服好聞又舒服。

4. job 工作。
Come to think of it, maybe I should find a job that has something to do with
clothes. 想來我也許應該找一個 跟衣服有關的工作。

vocabulary.postscript:
還記得今天的單字嗎?
wash洗 dry弄乾 clean乾淨的 job工作

quiz.question: 1. Who does the man help?
options: a. Police officers who have a home
b. People who do not have a home
c. People who have a home

quiz.question: 2. Where did the man put the washing machines and clothes dryers?
options: b. On top of the bus

quiz.question: 3. What does the man want now?

content.en: Have you seen U.S. money? Like Taiwan's money, U.S. money is called "dollar." One U.S. dollar has 100 cents.
content.tw: 你有看過美國的錢幣嗎？美國錢叫做美元，一美元等於一百美分。

content.en: The one-cent coin is called a penny. The penny looks a lot like Taiwan's one-dollar coin. The two coins are brown. They are about the same size.
content.tw: 一美分硬幣看起來很像台灣的一元硬幣，都是咖啡色的，大小也差不多。

content.en: Some people like coins very much. They try to get new coins, old coins, or rare coins. Rare means there are almost no others. The U.S. stopped making pennies in 2025. So the last pennies are new and rare.
content.tw: 有一些人非常喜歡收集硬幣。他們會到處找新的、舊的，或是很稀有的硬幣。美國2025年停止製造一美分硬幣，所以最後出廠的硬幣不但稀有，也是全新的。

content.en: The U.S. sold the last pennies. Pennies were made for 232 years, so it sold 232 sets. One set has three pennies. Let's see… That is 696 pennies.
content.tw: 美國把最後一批一美分硬幣拿出來賣。一美分硬幣已經生產了 232 年，所以他們賣了232 套，一套有3枚硬幣，一共是 696 枚。

content.en: But one penny in each set is made of gold! That is very rare!
content.tw: 但是，每一套硬幣裡面都有一枚是用黃金做的！這些就非常稀有了。

content.en: So how much did these 696 pennies sell for? They sold for 530 million Taiwan dollars! Whoa!
content.tw: 結果這些套幣到底賣了多少錢? 總共賣了五億三千萬台幣！

content.en: Goodbye, new pennies!
content.tw: 再見了，新的一美分硬幣！

vocabulary.preface: 一分錢的美元硬幣，去年十一月停止生產，最後一批特製的套幣也賣光了。

content.text:
1. gold 黃金。
I found a gold coin in my drawer. 我在我的抽屜裡找到一枚金幣。
Real gold? 真的黃金嗎?
That’s what it says. 它上面是這麼說的。
I’m pretty sure it’s made of gold. 我確定是黃金做的。

2. size 尺寸。
It's small. 它很小。
The size of one Taiwanese dollar. 大小跟台幣一塊錢一樣。
Still, it’s gold! 但它是黃金!
Let me check my drawers too! 我也要看看我的抽屜!

3. dollar 元。
Should I sell it? 我應該賣掉嗎?
How much is it worth? 它值多少錢?
No idea. Perhaps five hundred Taiwan dollars? 不知道。也許五百塊台幣?

4. sell 賣。
A small coin that can sell for $500? 一枚小硬幣可以賣五百塊?

vocabulary.postscript: 請跟我一起讀單字。 gold黃金 size尺寸 dollar元 sell賣

quiz.question: 1. How many cents is one penny?
3. How much did the last U.S. pennies sell for?
